[package]
name = "bids-rust"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.70"
authors = ["Bradley Buchsbaum <brad.buchsbaum@gmail.com>"]
description = "Rust implementation of BIDS (Brain Imaging Data Structure) data management functionality"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/bids-rust"
documentation = "https://docs.rs/bids-rust"
homepage = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/bids-rust"
readme = "README.md"
categories = ["science", "data-structures", "parsing"]
keywords = ["bids", "neuroimaging", "data-science", "neuroscience", "fmri"]
exclude = ["tests/fixtures/*", "benches/data/*"]

[dependencies]
# Core data structures and collections
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
indexmap = "2.0"  # Ordered maps for entity preservation

# String processing and regex
regex = "1.10"
once_cell = "1.19"  # For compiled regex patterns

# File I/O and path handling
csv = "1.3"
walkdir = "2.4"
dirs = "5.0"

# Mathematical operations and PCA
nalgebra = { version = "0.32", features = ["serde-serialize"] }
num-traits = "0.2"

# Error handling
thiserror = "1.0"
anyhow = "1.0"

# Logging
log = "0.4"

# Optional parallel processing
rayon = { version = "1.8", optional = true }

# Optional dataframe support
polars = { version = "~0.49", default-features = false, features = ["lazy", "polars-io", "csv"], optional = true }

# Date/time handling for timestamps
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }

# URL handling for dataset downloading
url = "2.5"
reqwest = { version = "0.11", features = ["json"], optional = true }

# Compression for dataset downloads
flate2 = { version = "1.0", optional = true }
zip = { version = "0.6", optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
# Testing framework
criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }
proptest = "1.4"
tempfile = "3.8"
approx = "0.5"
pretty_assertions = "1.4"

# Test utilities
rstest = "0.18"
serial_test = "3.0"

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []
parallel = ["rayon"]
dataframes = ["polars"]
download = ["reqwest", "flate2", "zip"]
python-interop = []  # Future Python bindings

# Development and testing features
testing = []
benchmarks = []

[[bench]]
name = "bids_benchmarks"
harness = false

[[example]]
name = "quick_start"
path = "examples/quick_start.rs"

[[example]]
name = "basic_usage"
path = "examples/basic_usage.rs"

[[example]]
name = "search_api"
path = "examples/search_api.rs"

[[example]]
name = "derivatives_usage"
path = "examples/derivatives_usage.rs"

[[example]]
name = "file_io"
path = "examples/file_io.rs"

[[example]]
name = "pca_analysis"
path = "examples/pca_analysis.rs"

[[example]]
name = "validate_against_r"
path = "examples/validate_against_r.rs"

[profile.release]
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
panic = "abort"

[profile.dev]
debug = true
overflow-checks = true

[profile.test]
debug = true

[profile.bench]
debug = true
lto = "thin"

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
all-features = true
rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]

# Workspace configuration for potential future sub-crates
# [workspace]
# members = [".", "bids-derive"]
# resolver = "2"

# Example workspace member for procedural macros (future)
# [workspace.dependencies]
# syn = "2.0"
# quote = "1.0"
# proc-macro2 = "1.0"

# BIDS-Rust Testing Guide

## Testing with Real BIDS Datasets

The test suite includes support for testing against real BIDS example datasets from the [bids-examples repository](https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-examples).

### Setup

1. Create a cache directory for BIDS examples:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/.cache/bids-rust-test-data/bids-examples
   ```

2. Download example datasets:
   ```bash
   cd ~/.cache/bids-rust-test-data/bids-examples
   
   # Download specific datasets
   git clone --depth 1 --filter=blob:none --sparse https://github.com/bids-standard/bids-examples
   cd bids-examples
   git sparse-checkout init --cone
   git sparse-checkout set ds000001 ds000002 ds000003 synthetic
   
   # Move datasets to parent directory
   mv ds* synthetic ../
   cd ..
   rm -rf bids-examples
   ```

3. Run tests:
   ```bash
   cargo test test_real_datasets
   ```

### Test Features

- **Automatic skipping**: Tests skip gracefully if datasets aren't available
- **Instructions**: Provides download instructions when datasets are missing
- **Temporary copies**: Tests work on copies to avoid modifying cached data
- **Multiple datasets**: Tests can validate across different dataset types

### Available Test Datasets

- `ds000001`: Single task, multiple runs
- `ds000002`: Multiple tasks  
- `ds000003`: Rhyme judgment task
- `ds000117`: Multi-echo dataset
- `synthetic`: Synthetic dataset with multiple modalities
- `ieeg_visual`: iEEG example
- `genetics_ukbb`: Genetics data

### Writing Tests with Real Data

```rust
use bids_rust::{BidsProject, Result};

#[test]
fn test_with_real_dataset() -> Result<()> {
    // This macro handles dataset availability checking
    let dataset_path = require_example_dataset!("ds000001");
    
    let project = BidsProject::new(&dataset_path)?;
    // ... test code ...
    
    Ok(())
}
```

### CI/CD Considerations

- Keep test datasets small (< 100MB) for CI
- Use the `is_ci_friendly()` method to check dataset size
- Consider using synthetic data for most tests
- Reserve real dataset tests for integration testing

## Summary

# Run all real dataset tests
